name: Ginger Bug
subtitle: Wild-fermented ginger starter for natural sodas
blrNote: Bengaluru is exceptional for ginger bugs - fresh ginger from KR Market or any sabziwala is cheap and loaded with wild yeasts. At BLR temps, expect a healthy bug in 3–4 days. Use it to carbonate homemade sodas - lemon soda, kokum soda, sugarcane soda.

IN.where: Any sabziwala or KR Market - ₹20–40 per 100g
Use filtered or boiled-and-cooled water. Tap water chlorine can inhibit wild yeasts.

equipment.name: Cloth or loose lid
equipment.notes: Needs to breathe - don't seal airtight

tldr: Grated ginger + sugar + water. Feed daily. It starts bubbling. Now you have a wild yeast starter you can use to naturally carbonate any sweet liquid.

steps.title: Start the jar
steps.instruction: In a clean jar, combine 500ml unchlorinated water, 2 tbsp grated unpeeled ginger, and 2 tbsp sugar. Stir well. Cover with a cloth. Leave at room temperature.
tips: Unpeeled ginger has more wild yeast on the skin
Dechlorinate tap water by leaving it uncovered overnight
Buy organic ginger if possible - conventionally grown ginger is sometimes irradiated, which kills the wild yeast you need
steps.checkpoint: Jar is cloudy with ginger bits

steps.title: Feed daily
steps.instruction: Every day for 3–7 days, add 1 tbsp fresh grated ginger and 1 tbsp sugar. Stir vigorously and re-cover.
tips: Vigorous stirring incorporates oxygen and helps wild yeast populate faster
Feed at the same time each day - consistency keeps the microbial population growing steadily
If you miss a day, double the next feeding to compensate - the bug is forgiving
steps.checkpoint: Bubbles start appearing after day 2–3 in BLR

steps.title: Watch for activity
steps.instruction: Your bug is ready when it's actively bubbling, smells yeasty-gingery, and fizzes when stirred. In Bengaluru, typically 3–4 days. Taste - it should be slightly fizzy, tangy-sweet.
tips: Don't refrigerate until active - cold slows wild yeast capture
Hold the jar up to light - you should see a steady stream of tiny bubbles rising like champagne
If nothing happens after 7 days, the ginger was likely irradiated - start over with a different source
steps.checkpoint: Consistent bubbling within minutes of stirring, fizzy taste

steps.title: Maintain or use
steps.instruction: Use ¼ cup of bug to ferment 1 litre of sweet liquid (ginger ale, lemon soda, etc.) for 2–3 days. Replenish what you used with water + ginger + sugar. Keep feeding to maintain indefinitely.
tips: Refrigerate and feed once a week if not using often
Bring back to room temp and feed daily for 2 days before using from the fridge
Strain out old ginger pieces monthly and replace with fresh - spent ginger slows the culture down
steps.checkpoint: Bug is a perpetual fermentation engine

images.caption: Ginger Bug
images.attribution: Photo by Mockup Graphics on Unsplash
images.alt: Ginger Bug - Wild-fermented ginger starter for natural sodas

culturalContext.story: Before commercial yeast and carbonation, people around the world maintained wild ferment starters to make fizzy drinks. The ginger bug is the simplest version - a living community of wild yeasts and bacteria captured from ginger's skin, sustained by sugar, used to naturally carbonate homemade sodas.
culturalContext.historicalNote: Traditional ginger beer (not the commercial version) was made this way in 18th and 19th century Britain. The "ginger beer plant" was a symbiotic community of yeast and bacteria kept alive for years and traded between households.
culturalContext.significance: The ginger bug is a gateway to natural carbonation. Once you have a healthy bug, you can turn any sweet liquid - fruit juice, herbal tea, kokum water, sugarcane juice - into a naturally fizzy probiotic drink. It's the South Asian equivalent of maintaining a sourdough starter.
culturalContext.funFact: The "ginger beer plant" was so prized in Victorian England that people traded it like a pet, giving portions to friends and family. Its actual microbial composition - a specific yeast (Saccharomyces florentinus) and bacteria (Lactobacillus) - was only identified in the 1980s.

thingsToAccountFor.title: Won't start if water is chlorinated
thingsToAccountFor.description: Tap water chlorine kills wild yeast. Boil and cool, or leave overnight uncovered to dechlorinate before using.

thingsToAccountFor.title: Mould vs. yeast foam
thingsToAccountFor.description: White bubbles and foam = good yeast activity. Fuzzy coloured growth = mould. If you see mould, discard and start again.

thingsToAccountFor.title: Very fast in BLR heat
thingsToAccountFor.description: At 28–30°C in summer, your bug can become very active within 48 hours. Taste before using - if very sour, it's over-fermented. Use less and top up sugar.

variations.name: Turmeric Bug
variations.description: Substitute half the ginger with fresh turmeric root. Same method. Adds anti-inflammatory compounds and a golden colour to sodas.

variations.name: Kokum Ginger Soda
variations.description: Use ¼ cup active bug to ferment 1L kokum extract + jaggery syrup for 2 days. Naturally fizzy, deeply Konkan.

variations.name: Lemon Ginger Ale
variations.description: Dissolve 100g sugar in 1L water, add ginger bug and fresh lime juice. Bottle and leave 2–3 days. Classic natural soda.
